title: impatiens inaperta var. viridis
intro: Impatiens inaperta var. viridis, also known as Balsam Apple or Impatiens, is a plant species belonging to the balsam family (Balsamaceae) and is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Impatiens inaperta var. viridis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and provide relief from skin irritations and wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are typically used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
